title: The Parameter Bottleneck
concept: Larger models require massive GPU memory, forcing engineers to split parameters across multiple graphics cards during training and inference.

exercises.prompt: Which hardware component is most commonly used to train and run large AI models because of its fast memory and parallel processing power-efficiently run modern AI models due to its fast parallel processing?
exercises.explain: Graphics processing units (GPUs) are specialized hardware chips designed with fast memory to handle the heavy math calculations required by AI models.

cards.title: Too Big for One Brain
cards.body: Have you ever tried to open a file that was too big for your computer's memory, causing it to freeze? Giant AI models face this every day. They are so massive that they cannot fit inside even the most expensive graphics cards!

cards.title: What is the Parameter Bottleneck?
cards.body: The Parameter Bottleneck happens when an AI model has so many settings (parameters) that its total size exceeds the memory (VRAM) of a single graphics card (GPU). To run the model, engineers must divide and conquer by splitting these parameters across several GPUs.

cards.title: The Lego Castle Split
cards.body: Imagine you are building a giant Lego castle that is too wide to fit on a single table. Instead of cramming it on one table and watching it fall, you place different sections of the castle on separate, connected tables. In AI, the castle sections are the model's parameters, and the tables are the GPUs.

cards.title: Real-World Scale
cards.body: Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4 contain hundreds of billions of parameters. A single high-end GPU might only hold 80 Gigabytes of data. Because the model files are hundreds of Gigabytes, they must be split across 8 or more GPUs just to load them into memory!

cards.title: Myth: More GPUs are Just for Speed
cards.body: Many people think that adding more GPUs just makes training faster. While true for smaller models, for giant models, adding more GPUs is a basic requirement just to fit the model in memory so it can run at all!

cards.title: Memory Limits Visualized
cards.body: We can compare what happens when a model fits on a single card versus when it overflows and must be distributed.

right.text: Giant model exceeds memory, requiring parameters to be split across multiple GPUs.

left.text: Small model fits entirely inside one GPU's memory.

exercises.prompt: What happens during a parameter bottleneck?

options.text: The GPU runs out of battery power.
The model cannot fit into the GPU's memory.

exercises.explain: When a model's parameters exceed a single GPU's memory limit, it causes a memory bottleneck, requiring the model to be divided.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you train a giant AI model on a very small dataset, you will never face a parameter bottleneck.
exercises.explain: Even if a dataset is tiny, a massive model with billions of parameters still requires huge GPU memory just to load its weights.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of the parameter bottleneck.
exercises.sentence: The bottleneck occurs because a single GPU cannot hold all the model's parameters at once.
exercises.explain: Giant AI models have billions of parameters, which require massive amounts of memory, forcing engineers to split them across cards.

exercises.prompt: Two of these items are parts of the AI model that engineers split across GPUs to avoid a bottleneck. Select the one that is NOT split this way.

options.text: The layers of the neural network
The user's text prompt: "Write a poem about bees"

exercises.explain: The prompt generated by the human ("Write a poem...") is just text input, not a structural model component like weights or layers. The model weights and neural layers represent the actual parameter data that must be split.

exercises.prompt: Your team is building a massive 100-billion parameter AI model, but it is too large to fit on your best GPU. How do you solve this parameter bottleneck?

options.text: Split the model's parameters across multiple GPUs so each holds a piece.
Buy cheaper GPUs with less memory but higher speeds.
Compress all text inputs into tiny codes so they fit on one GPU.
Train the model only on CPUs instead of GPUs.

exercises.explain: Model parallelism (or parameter splitting) is specifically designed to solve the parameter bottleneck, where a single model's weights are too large to fit onto the memory of a single GPU.
